---
title: "Building Capital"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: true
jupyter: capital_1
---

Ok I've been starting to build capital. I have a few simple functions and a simple notebook. I convert .svs images (the standart histopathological format) into ome.zarr fiels (the standart comp bio image format). Then I make some simple plots and test a masking function.

# Read and plot the HE image with reduced resolution

```{python}
%matplotlib inline

import capital_1 as cptl
import pandas as pd

metadf = cptl.IO.get_metadf()

import zarr
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

zarr_path = metadf.iloc[0]['zarr_path']

z = zarr.open(str(zarr_path), mode='r')
low_res_arr = z[::4,::4,:]

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 10))
plt.imshow(low_res_arr)
plt.show()
```

#plot a zoomed in crop
```{python}
%matplotlib inline
plt.figure(figsize=(20, 20))
plt.imshow(z[16000:17000,16000:17000,:])
plt.axis('off')
plt.show()
```

#Test the masking function and plot the mask

```{python}
%matplotlib inline
from skimage.color import rgb2hsv

mask = cptl.IO.get_tissue_mask(low_res_arr)

# Plot the proof
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 6))

axes[0].imshow(low_res_arr)
axes[0].set_title("Original RGB")
axes[0].axis('off')

# Show the raw saturation channel (Tissue will be bright, background dark)
axes[1].imshow(rgb2hsv(low_res_arr)[:, :, 1], cmap='gray')
axes[1].set_title("Saturation Channel")
axes[1].axis('off')

# Show the final boolean mask
axes[2].imshow(mask, cmap='gray')
axes[2].set_title("Otsu Binary Mask")
axes[2].axis('off')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
